source.title: Flash Fire During Gas Compressor Maintenance - West Delta Block 105 Platform E

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - West Delta Block 105

actions_taken: Mechanic activated Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system
Mechanic retrieved and used fire extinguisher to extinguish fire
Platform shut in pending root cause analysis
BSEE granted permission to pressure test fuel gas regulator on 29-NOV-2020 at 1718 hours
BSEE granted permission to repair compressor and restart platform on 01-DEC-2020
Fieldwood released Safety Bulletin to all Gulf of Mexico personnel on 01-DEC-2020
Replaced gaskets on all 16 cylinder head fuel injection line flanges
Inspected and replaced ignition secondary wires as needed
Added fuel injection flange gasket replacement to annual inspection checklist
Educated maintenance personnel on risks of disconnecting ignition wires while equipment running
event.summary: On 29-NOV-2020 at 0657 hours, a flash fire occurred during maintenance troubleshooting of a gas compressor engine (CBA-2030) at the West Delta Block 105 Platform E. A mechanic was troubleshooting a non-firing No. 6 left bank power cylinder. When the mechanic disconnected the secondary spark plug wire from the ignition coil, a spark was introduced into an area containing leaked fuel gas at explosive concentrations. The gas ignited, causing a flash fire. The root cause was a cut gasket on the fuel valve jumper line that allowed gas to leak. The mechanic immediately activated the ESD system, descended the catwalk, and extinguished the fire with a fire extinguisher. No injuries, pollution, or property damage occurred. The platform was shut in until repairs were completed and permission to restart was granted by BSEE on 01-DEC-2020. The probable cause was identified as inadequate hazard analysis in the Job Safety Analysis (JSA), failure to use a gas detector, and failure to utilize a hot work permit.
recommendations: Utilize gas detector to confirm area is gas-free prior to troubleshooting suspected ignition-related issues where equipment is still running or online
Utilize hot work permit program when troubleshooting suspected ignition issues with fire watch present
Improve pre-job planning and discussion between mechanic and Person In Charge (PIC) signing the JSA
Educate maintenance personnel on risks of disconnecting ignition wires while equipment is running
Ensure JSA is task-specific and identifies all potential hazards including gas leaks and ignition sources
key_phrases: cut gasket on fuel valve jumper line

threats.name: Fuel gas leak from failed gasket on fuel valve jumper line
Spark generation from disconnecting spark plug wire from ignition coil
Inadequate hazard identification in Job Safety Analysis
Absence of gas detection prior to introducing ignition source

consequences.name: Flash fire in compressor engine area

controls.name: Fuel system gasket integrity and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Prevent fuel gas leak from cylinder head fuel injection line
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering barrier to prevent gas release

supporting_text: The No. 6 left bank cylinder head fuel injection line had gas leaking due to a failed gasket where the machined flange bolts to the cylinder head
The findings indicated that the cause of the gas leak was a cut gasket on the fuel valve jumper line

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for maintenance tasks
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and controls specific to maintenance task
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify and communicate hazards before work begins

supporting_text: The JSA was generic and the list of 'Potential Safety Hazards and Controls' was not specific to the task at hand
There was no mention of a potential gas leak or ignition source
BSEE found the probable cause of the incident to be that Fieldwood's management systems inadequately identified hazards

controls.name: Gas detection prior to introducing ignition sources
controls.barrier_role: Detect presence of hazardous gas before spark introduction
controls.lod_basis: Detection barrier to prevent ignition in presence of flammable gas

supporting_text: A gas detector was not used to see if any gas may have been present prior to introduction of an ignition source into a classified area
A gas detector was NOT used to see if any gas might have been present prior to introducing an ignition source into a classified area

controls.name: Hot work permit program
controls.barrier_role: Control and authorize work involving potential ignition sources
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to manage ignition source introduction

supporting_text: A hot work permit was not utilized
Utilize hot work permit program when troubleshooting suspected ignition issues and have a fire watch present

controls.name: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system activation
controls.barrier_role: Automatically shut down equipment and sound alarm upon fire detection
controls.lod_basis: Automated response to limit escalation of fire incident

supporting_text: The mechanic immediately climbed down the catwalk, activated the Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system, and grabbed a fire extinguisher. The ESD automatically sounded the fire alarm

controls.name: Fire extinguisher availability and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Provide means to extinguish incipient fire
controls.lod_basis: First response tool to suppress fire before escalation

supporting_text: He then went back to the compressor and extinguished the fire

BSEE found that Fieldwood's response to fire was adequate and followed regulations. The fire extinguisher was in working order and the inspection was up-to-date, the location of the fire extinguisher was close enough to fight an incipient fire

controls.name: Personnel training on ignition wire disconnection hazards
controls.barrier_role: Educate maintenance personnel on risks of disconnecting ignition wires while equipment running
controls.lod_basis: Knowledge-based control to prevent unsafe work practices

supporting_text: When the mechanic loosened and disconnected the secondary spark plug wire from the coil, the cylinder caught fire
Educate maintenance personnel of risk of disconnecting ignition wires while equipment is running

controls.name: Pre-job planning and discussion between mechanic and Person In Charge
controls.barrier_role: Ensure thorough planning and hazard discussion before maintenance work
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to improve hazard awareness and work planning

supporting_text: Pre-job planning needs to be more thought out and discussed by both the mechanic and the PIC signing the JSA

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
